steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Transitions question. The previous sentence grants that Alkaid ranks only 40th (less bright than Gamma Velorum), and the blank sentence then offers an explanation for Alkaid's brightness via proximity. The transition must concede that prior point before pivoting to the explanation.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Indeed,’: signals agreement or reinforcement, not a concession followed by an explanation. It would suggest the next sentence simply confirms the previous one, which it does not. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Granted,’: signals a concession ("I accept the previous point") and smoothly introduces the compensating explanation that follows. Matches the passage's logic exactly. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘As a result,’: signals a consequence or effect. Alkaid's proximity is not a result of its ranking; this forces a false causal link. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Similarly,’: signals that the next idea parallels the previous one. The passage contrasts Alkaid with Gamma Velorum rather than drawing a parallel. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
